common.tagline: Building leaders with global and innovative purpose
common.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.

hero.eyebrow: Youth Leadership Program · La Guajira, Colombia
hero.title: Building leaders
hero.subtitle: We develop the leadership skills of students from third to eleventh grade, preparing them to transform their communities with innovation, ethics, and an international vision.

mision.title: Why we exist
mision.body: To promote continuous development of leadership skills in students and strengthen their talents, preparing them to achieve a better attitude, aptitude (academic excellence) and a global role in society; guided by entrepreneurship, innovation, social awareness, and respect for the environment.
mision.focusValues: Transformational leadership · Social awareness · Creativity · Innovation
mision.titleLine1: We want to change the world

vision.title: Where we are heading
vision.body: By 2030, the Big Family program will be recognized nationally and internationally as an innovative initiative through which participating students enhance their leadership within a framework of educational excellence, values, and principles.
vision.para1: We aspire to expand our school leadership network to have a presence in more than 50 countries, connecting more than 5,000 students who generate a positive impact in their communities.
vision.para2: We seek for The Big Leader certification to be a clear and reliable signal of real skills — so that a company or organization sees it and immediately understands the value of the person: their ability to lead, work as a team, make decisions, and generate impact.
vision.animatedWords: By 2036, The Big Family will be recognized globally as a *benchmark* of excellence in *leadership.*

historia.title: Since 2015
historia.body: The Big Family program began in August 2015 with 15 students in second, third, and fourth grade. Today it has 98 active participants. It is a continuous program in which students receive training and take action every year.
historia.subtitle: How Big Family was born
historia.secSubtitle: The beginning of a youth leadership movement.
historia.secBody: In 2015, Luis Barrios founded Big Family with the conviction that the youth of La Guajira had the potential to transform their region. What started as a pilot project in one school has today become a leadership network that impacts hundreds of students across Colombia and beyond.
historia.secTitle: An idea born in La Guajira.

metodologia.title: How we build leaders
metodologia.subtitle: Four integrated components that develop leadership from the inside out.

bigLeader.title: The Big Leader
bigLeader.subtitle: Personalized leadership profile
bigLeader.body: Personalized training adapted to each student, focused on intrapersonal and interpersonal skills. Includes coaching sessions that guide students toward their life projects.

leadersGame.title: The Leader's Game
leadersGame.subtitle: Challenge-based learning
leadersGame.body: A station-based challenge game where teams accumulate points for correct answers. By identifying each participant's strengths and growth areas, targeted high-intensity workshops can be designed.

greatVenture.title: The Great Venture
greatVenture.subtitle: Personal strategic roadmap
greatVenture.body: An application with an algorithm that helps students define their direction and priorities to achieve their great enterprise as innovative global entrepreneurs. It guides them in selecting their core goal, empowering beliefs, appreciative paradigm, power team, and action plans, using the Hoshin Kanri matrix.

kashi.title: Kashi
kashi.subtitle: Educational social network
kashi.body: Kashi is a Wayuu word meaning "moon". This initiative emerged from School Leadership Day, when students had the opportunity to act as teachers, administrators, and facilitators. Their strengths in specific areas are shared with peers who wish to learn about a topic.

aprendizaje.title: Your leadership profile
aprendizaje.subtitle: The program adapts every experience to your unique leadership archetype.
aprendizaje.step1Body: You complete the Big Five test (BFI-44) when you join the program. 44 scientifically calibrated questions.
aprendizaje.step2Body: The algorithm generates your leadership pentagon and places you in one of the 5 archetypes of the Big Leader Model.
aprendizaje.step3Body: Modules, reflections, and your Great Venture are personalized according to your strengths and growth areas.
aprendizaje.discoverCta: What kind of leader are you?
aprendizaje.stepRouteDesc: Modules, reflections and deliverables personalized for you

visionario.name: The Visionary
visionario.description: You see patterns where others see chaos. Your mind connects distant ideas and builds futures that don't yet exist. The program gives you the tools to ground that vision in concrete projects.
visionario.example: You design a social entrepreneurship project connecting your school with partners in 3 countries.

conector.name: The Connector
conector.description: You build bridges between people. You know how to read the room, mediate conflicts, and make every voice count. Your leadership multiplies the team's potential.
conector.example: You lead your school's team at the inter-institutional meeting and ensure everyone participates.

ejecutor.name: The Executor
ejecutor.description: You turn plans into reality. Where others see obstacles, you see steps. Your consistency and rigor are the engine that drives projects to completion.
ejecutor.example: You execute your IDEMR capstone with a detailed plan, complete all stages and present measurable results.

catalizador.name: The Catalyst
catalizador.description: Your energy is contagious. You inspire others to move, to dare, to believe. The program channels that force toward a lasting impact in your community.
catalizador.example: You organize Leadership Day at your school and bring together 200 students from 5 institutions.

guardian.name: The Guardian
guardian.description: You are the team's anchor. Your presence creates psychological safety so others can take risks. The program develops your ability to lead through consistency and values.
guardian.example: You are the integrity reference at your school — coordinators and peers trust your judgment.

etica.title: Ethics
etica.body: We act with integrity in every decision, building trust from within.

compromiso.title: Commitment
compromiso.body: We keep our promises and give our best in every action.

trascendencia.title: Transcendence
trascendencia.body: We seek to leave a positive mark beyond our generation.

concienciaSocial.title: Social Awareness
concienciaSocial.body: We recognize our impact on the community and act responsibly.

innovacion.title: Innovation
innovacion.body: We seek creative solutions to the challenges of our environment.

creatividad.title: Creativity
creatividad.body: We explore new ways of thinking and doing to generate unique value.

alumni.title: Leaders who have already made their mark
alumni.subtitle: Program graduates who today lead at universities and organizations around the world.
alumni.card2Program: Bachelor in Transformational Leadership and Social Impact
alumni.card3Role: Vice President of Latin Students · Concordia University, Canada

acreditaciones.title: Recognized by the world's best educational organizations
acreditaciones.sub: International organizations of educational excellence have recognized Big Family as an innovative program with real social impact.

cognia.name: Cognia
cognia.description: Recognized the program as innovative and socially relevant during its accreditation visit.

ib.name: IB Americas Conference
ib.description: The program was presented to the international educational community of the International Baccalaureate.

triAssociation.name: Tri-Association
triAssociation.description: Highlighted the importance of the program in 2024 and selected it to participate in the TRIHEROES event in May 2025.

iberoamericano.name: Congreso Iberoamericano
iberoamericano.description: The proposal 'How to achieve a successful leadership development process in schools' won third place.

fundador.title: Luis Hernando Barrios
fundador.subtitle: Founder, The Big Family Program
fundador.bio: An educator with more than a decade training leaders in La Guajira. His vision of transformational leadership with global purpose has led the program to be recognized in 10 countries.

education.label: Education
education.degree1: M.S. Multidisciplinary Studies — University at Buffalo
education.degree2: Leadership, Creativity, Innovation and Entrepreneurship — Javeriana, MIT, Uninorte, Unisabana

red.title: Colombia at the center of the world
red.subtitle: Active partnerships with institutions in 10 countries that broaden the horizons of our students.
red.growSubtitle: Connecting leaders from La Guajira with allies around the world.
red.modalBody: Big Family maintains an active connection with educational institutions in {country}, expanding the youth leadership network at an international level.

equipo.title: Who trains our leaders
equipo.body: The training team is made up of volunteer facilitators specialized in diverse areas: school community parents, teachers, graduates, corporate staff and executives, independent trainers, university professors, and foundation representatives.
equipo.founder1Bio: Builds and maintains the technology platform that makes The Big Leader certification possible.
equipo.founder2Bio: The face and voice of the program. Leads the content strategy and communication of Big Family's impact.
equipo.founder3Bio: Structure and organization of the entire program. Ensures every piece of the system works coherently.

testimonial.quote: Thank you very much Mr. Barrios for giving our students this opportunity. They greatly enjoyed the chance to interact with students from around the world, and their feedback has been excellent. We hope they can incorporate the lessons learned to become transformational leaders.
testimonial.author: Jonathan Smith

colegios.title: 8 institutions in La Guajira
colegios.tickerSub: From La Guajira, building leaders who transform Colombia.

cert.title: An achievement that opens doors.
cert.para: Upon completing the 12 modules and the capstone project, each student receives The Big Leader certification — recognized by Cognia, IB and Tri-Association.

cta.title: Your leadership starts here.
cta.subtitle: Join hundreds of young people already transforming their communities.

faq.title: Everything you need to know.
faq.q1: Who can participate in the program?
faq.a1: Students from third to eleventh grade at participating schools in La Guajira.
faq.a2: No. The program is completely free for students at participating schools.
faq.q3: How much time does it require?
faq.a3: 40 annual hours in extracurricular format — in-person, virtual, and asynchronous.
faq.q4: What do I get upon completion?
faq.a4: A verifiable QR certificate, a public leadership portfolio, and institutional recognition.
faq.a5: Your coordinator gives you an access code. With that code you create your account on the platform.

t1.quote: The program changed the way I see the world. I learned that to lead is to serve.
t1.name: Big Family Student

t2.quote: Thanks to the program, I represented Colombia at an international conference.
t2.name: Big Family Student

t3.quote: The leadership test helped me understand my real strengths.
t3.name: Big Family Student

about.title: Leaders transforming La Guajira
about.para1: Big Family is a youth leadership program that accompanies students from 8 schools in La Guajira, Colombia, on their path to becoming agents of change in their communities.
about.para2: Through modules, real projects and mentorship, young people develop the skills and vision they need to lead the future of their region.

successStoriesSection.subtitle: Students who transformed their communities through leadership.

footer.brandDesc: Youth leadership program connecting students from La Guajira with a global network of impact.

step1.titleDefault: What is your big dream as a leader?
step1.titleVisionario: Your profile shows you think big. What is the vision that keeps you up at night?
step1.titleConstructor: Your relationships are your strength. What is the dream you share with the people who matter to you?
step1.titleResiliente: Your discipline is your foundation. What dream would you pursue even when things get hard?
step1.titleConector: Your energy activates people. What is the dream you want to build together with others?
step1.titleEstratega: Your long-term vision is your advantage. What goal do you set for the next 5 years?
step1.subtitle: You don't have to solve the world. Start with something concrete you can pursue over the next few years.
step1.placeholder: E.g.: Create a leadership program at my school that reaches the whole region...

step2.titleDefault: What beliefs give you strength to pursue that goal?
step2.titleVinculo: What beliefs about people give you strength when teamwork gets hard?
step2.titleAccion: What beliefs give you the push to act even when things are not fully ready?
step2.titleNorte: What beliefs keep you oriented when the vision gets blurry?
step2.subtitle: Beliefs are the deep convictions that sustain you when things get complicated.
step2.placeholder: E.g.: I believe anyone can lead from where they are. I believe my community deserves better opportunities...

step3.eyebrow: STEP 3 · HOW I SEE THE WORLD
step3.title: What is your lens for seeing the world?
step3.subtitle: Your paradigm is the way you interpret what happens. A leader with an appreciative paradigm sees opportunities where others see problems.
step3.placeholder: E.g.: I see every obstacle as information. I believe people want to grow if someone accompanies them...

step4.eyebrow: STEP 4 · THE PEOPLE WHO SUPPORT ME
step4.titleDefault: Who are the key people on your path?
step4.titleVisionario: Your profile shows you tend to go it alone. Who complements your big-picture thinking with concrete execution?
step4.titleEstratega: Your strength is strategic. Who helps you connect with people and bring ideas down to earth?
step4.subtitle: Your power team is the people who complement you, challenge you, and support you.
step4.rolePlaceholder: Role in your life (e.g.: gives me honest feedback)

step5.title: What are your first 3 steps?
step5.subtitle: Not the perfect plan — the first concrete moves toward your goal.

diaLiderazgo.subtitle: 8 schools. 1 day. A project that changes your community.
diaLiderazgo.aboutText: A day where students from La Guajira document and present their community leadership projects. Each project is evaluated by coordinators from Big Family's The Big Leader program.
diaLiderazgo.finalSubtitle: Complete your project and submit it before May 16
diaLiderazgo.footerTagline: The Big Leader · Leadership Day 2026 · La Guajira
